questions.question: Explain the four values of the Agile Manifesto.
keyPoints: Individuals and interactions over processes and tools
Working software over comprehensive documentation
Customer collaboration over contract negotiation
Responding to change over following a plan

questions.question: Differentiate between Product Backlog and Sprint Backlog.
keyPoints: Product Backlog contains all desired features/features prioritized by Product Owner
Sprint Backlog is subset of Product Backlog items selected for current Sprint
Product Backlog is dynamic and changes throughout project
Sprint Backlog is fixed for the duration of the Sprint

questions.question: What are the three roles in Scrum framework? Explain each briefly.
keyPoints: Product Owner - manages product backlog, defines features, prioritizes work
Scrum Master - facilitates Scrum process, removes impediments, coaches team
Development Team - cross-functional, self-organizing, delivers increments

questions.question: Describe the key differences between Agile and Waterfall methodologies.
keyPoints: Waterfall is sequential; Agile is iterative
Waterfall has fixed requirements; Agile welcomes changes
Waterfall delivers at end; Agile delivers incrementally
Waterfall has extensive documentation; Agile focuses on working software
Waterfall has rigid planning; Agile adapts to change

questions.question: Explain the purpose and benefits of Daily Stand-up meetings in Agile.
keyPoints: 15-minute time-boxed meeting for team synchronization
Team members share: yesterday's work, today's plan, any blockers
Helps identify impediments early
Improves team communication and transparency
Enables quick decision making

questions.question: What is a User Story? Explain the format and give an example.
keyPoints: User Story is a short description of a feature from user's perspective
Format: 'As a [role], I want [goal] so that [benefit]'
Example: As a student, I want to view my attendance so that I can track my class participation
User Stories are independent, negotiable, valuable, estimable, small, and testable (INVEST)

questions.question: Explain the Scrum framework in detail. Include all Scrum events, artifacts, and roles with their purposes.
keyPoints: Roles: Product Owner, Scrum Master, Development Team
Events: Sprint (2-4 weeks), Sprint Planning, Daily Scrum, Sprint Review, Sprint Retrospective
Artifacts: Product Backlog, Sprint Backlog, Increment
Product Backlog - prioritized list of requirements
Sprint Backlog - items selected for current Sprint
Increment - sum of all completed items + previous increments
Each event has specific purpose and time-box

questions.question: Discuss the 12 principles behind the Agile Manifesto and explain how they influence modern software development.
keyPoints: Customer satisfaction through early and continuous delivery
Welcome changing requirements, even late in development
Deliver working software frequently
Business people and developers must work together daily
Build projects around motivated individuals
Face-to-face conversation is the most efficient communication
Working software is the primary measure of progress
Sustainable development pace
Continuous attention to technical excellence and good design
Simplicity - maximize work not done
Self-organizing teams produce best designs
Regular adaptation to changing circumstances

questions.question: Explain Kanban method in Agile. How does it differ from Scrum? Discuss Kanban board, WIP limits, and flow optimization.
keyPoints: Kanban is a visual workflow management method
Uses a board with columns: To Do, In Progress, Done
Work In Progress (WIP) limits restrict items in each column
Focuses on continuous delivery and flow optimization
Differences from Scrum: No fixed sprints, no prescribed roles, evolutionary change
Pull-based system - work is pulled when capacity is available
Metrics: Lead time, cycle time, throughput
Enables teams to start with existing workflow and improve gradually

questions.question: What is Sprint Retrospective? Explain its purpose, participants, and techniques used. How does it help in continuous improvement?
keyPoints: Sprint Retrospective is held at end of every Sprint
Purpose: Team reflects on past Sprint to improve process
Participants: Entire Scrum Team (PO, SM, Development Team)
Time-box: Maximum 3 hours for 1-month Sprint
Techniques: Start-Stop-Continue, Mad-Sad-Glad, 4Ls (Liked, Learned, Lacked, Longed for)
Team identifies what went well, what didn't, and action items for improvement
Helps create a culture of continuous learning and adaptation
Scrum Master facilitates and ensures productive discussion
